instruction:
Add comprehensive edge case tests for `c_across`.


Existing tests for reference:
```r

```


The existing tests cover basic functionality. Add tests for:
1. NULL inputs to each argument
2. Empty vectors/strings: character(0), "", integer(0)
3. Boundary values: 0, 1, -1, NA, NaN, Inf
4. Invalid input types (passing numeric where character expected, etc.)
5. Missing arguments (use expect_error with class matching)
6. Very long inputs (strings > 1000 chars, vectors > 10000 elements)
context:
Package: dplyr v1.2.0.9000
Description: A Grammar of Data Manipulation
Function: c_across
Parameters: cols
Test patterns used: test_that
Expectations used: expect_identical, expect_snapshot

Function Code:
```r
c_across <- function(cols) {
  mask <- peek_mask()
  cols <- enquo(cols)

  if (quo_is_missing(cols)) {
    c_across_missing_cols_deprecate_warn()
    cols <- quo_set_expr(cols, expr(everything()))
  }

  vars <- c_across_setup(!!cols, mask = mask)

  cols <- mask$current_cols(vars)
  vec_c(!!!cols, .name_spec = zap())
}
```
